title: Campus Prayer
content: Campus Prayer (Sanskrit): OM. Whole is that (Brahman), whole also is this (world). From whole comes the whole; take whole from whole; (yet) whole remains. OM. Whatever exists in this world is to be enveloped by God (The Ruler). By renouncing it (the world) thou mayst enjoy. Do not covet the riches of anyone. I bow down to Madhava, the Bliss Eternal; to Him whose grace enables the dumb to speak (and) the lame to go across a mountain. OM. May Sarasvati, the Divine protect us by her strength-giving favours, she who is rich in strength-giving favours, the projector of our thoughts. Sarasvati, who inspires hymns and promotes pious thoughts, brings about our sacrifice. OM. From the unreal lead me to the real. From darkness leads me to light. From death lead me to immortality. OM. May God protect us both at the same time; at the same time support us both. May both of us at the same time apply (our) strength. May our learning be illustrious. May there be no hatred (between us). May all here be happy. May all be free from disease. May all see well-being. May nobody experience misery. OM. Peace, Peace, Peace.

title: Somaiya Vidyavihar University Overview
content: Somaiya Vidyavihar University (SVU) is an educational institution of academic brilliance, embodying over 80 years of educational leadership and innovation. Established as a private university by the Somaiya Vidyavihar University, Mumbai Act of 2019, it thrives on a sprawling, green, 50-acre campus in the heart of Mumbai. Here, more than 10,000 students immerse themselves in a wide spectrum of disciplines—from Management, Engineering, and Design to Humanities and the Sciences. With its foundation laid by the visionary Padmabhushan K. J. Somaiya, the University offers a transformative educational experience where students enjoy the intellectual freedom to explore diverse 'major-minor' combinations. This distinctive approach encourages academic exploration and lifelong connections with a global alum network that extends far beyond the campus. Recognised under the UGC Act 1956, SVU opens doors to global opportunities. Students benefit from an advanced incubation centre that nurtures budding entrepreneurs, robust placement services that bridge the gap between education and industry, and world-class on-campus accommodation. The guiding ethos of 'Freedom of Possibilities' empowers students to create their path to excellence, growth, and self-discovery. With an ambition to craft an institution that is both unapologetically Indian and globally renowned for research, Somaiya Vidyavihar stands tall. Its 40 affiliate institutions educate over 39,000 students, supported by a distinguished faculty of 1,500, spread across its two main campuses at Vidyavihar and Ayurvihar in Sion, Mumbai. Additionally, smaller campuses across rural Maharashtra, Karnataka, Gujarat, and Madhya Pradesh extend the reach of its academic prowess. These educational institutions offer various undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral programmes, along with vocational training and schools that instruct in English, Gujarati, and Marathi from pre-primary to higher secondary. At SVU, education is not merely imparted but lived. It is an ecosystem where knowledge is preserved, shared, and created anew—fuelled by disciplined commitment to research, consultancy, and service. The University is celebrated not only for its excellence in Science, Technology, Engineering, Medicine, Management, Social Sciences, and the Humanities but also for its unique emphasis on studying the diverse faiths and cultures of India. SVU is a sanctuary for innovation, intellectual curiosity, and leadership, inspiring students to transcend the ordinary and shape a future without limits.

title: K J Somaiya Institute of Management Overview
content: The K J Somaiya Institute of Management (KJSIM) in Mumbai stands as a distinguished business school, proudly affiliated with the esteemed Somaiya Vidyavihar University. Its inception dates to 1981, and over the years, it has risen to the ranks of the top 10 private business schools in India. KJSIM believes in enabling transformation journeys of individuals to value-based & purpose-driven leaders for building a better world. Nestled in a beautifully landscaped campus in the heart of the commercial capital of India, KJSIM acts as a perfect crucible for transformation of students. KJSIM offers core MBA, domain specific and multi-disciplinary programmes like MBA in Healthcare Management and MBA in Sports Management, Executive MBA, an MBA for working executives (online) and MCA programme in step with the requirements of the various sectors of the industry and growth opportunities in the economy. Apart from the regular programmes, the Institute offers customised and industry specific certificate and executive development programmes for government bodies, companies, defense personnel and NGOs. KJSIM also holds the coveted AACSB accreditation and has been recognised in India's 'Top 25 Business Schools'. With a razor-sharp goal of creating 'day-one ready graduates', the cornerstone of KJSIM's strength lies in the extensive industry collaborations and 15000+ global alumni base. It offers students opportunities such as guest speaker sessions, summer internships, capstone projects and mentorship from industry experts that align with tomorrow's industry requirements. With 20+ collaborations with international universities, exchange and immersion opportunities are available for students and faculty. KJSIM is led by Director & Dean, Raman Ramachandran, Ph.D, former Chairman and Managing Director of BASF India, is a committed proponent of bridging the gap between industry expectations and graduate outcomes. He spearheads a team of seasoned faculty and dedicated staff members.

title: Vision, Mission, Values and Purpose
content: Vision: Our dream is to build a world-class research and teaching institution that is global in the reach of its ideas and universal in its service. Mission: To foster a spirit of inquiry, enable livelihoods, encourage innovations and create good citizenships. Values: Excellence: Striving for higher standards in all endeavours. Integrity: Upholding honesty and strong moral principles. Collaborative: Fostering teamwork and shared success. Curiosity: Encouraging continuous learning and exploration. Humility: Valuing modesty and recognizing the contributions of others. Purpose: Transforming, Together. Together, we enable transformation journeys of individuals to value-based and purpose-driven leaders for building a better world.

title: Vice-Chancellor's Message
content: Dear Students, It is with great pleasure that I extend a warm welcome to each one of you, as you embark on a transformative journey at our K J Somaiya Institute of Management (KJSIM). On behalf of the faculty, staff, and the entire Somaiya Vidyavihar University community, I congratulate you on your acceptance into our MBA programme. At KJSIM, we have built a legacy of excellence in business education, providing students with the knowledge, skills, and experiences necessary to thrive in a rapidly changing global business landscape. Our MBA programme is designed to challenge you intellectually, broaden your perspectives, and equip you with the tools to become effective leaders and change agents in the world of business. As you commence your MBA studies, I encourage you to engage in rigorous academic pursuits, collaborate with diverse peers, and learn from our renowned faculty who bring a wealth of expertise and industry experience to the classroom. At KJSIM, you can take full advantage of the resources and support available to you. Our dedicated career services team is here to assist you in shaping your career path, connecting you with internships and job opportunities, and offering guidance on professional development. Engage in extracurricular activities, join student clubs, and participate in various events and conferences in the University campus. Our faculty and staff are here to support you on every step of the way. As you commence this transformative chapter of your academic and professional lives, I am confident that you will emerge as leaders who will shape the future of business. I wish you all the best on this exciting journey and encourage you to make the most of every opportunity that comes your way.

title: Director's Message
content: Dear Students, It is my privilege to welcome you all to this prestigious institute with a legacy of more than 40 years of quality business education. As you start this new phase of your life in our institute, I want to acknowledge and share the unprecedented legacy of our founder, Padma Bhushan Shri Karamshi Jethabhai Somaiya. Today, we are here because of his vision and commitment to the philosophy: 'ज्ञानादेव हि तत्त्वदर्शनम्' which means 'Knowledge Alone Liberates'. Karamshiji was an exceptional entrepreneur with exceptional grit and determination. Hailing from Kutch region, he moved to Mumbai in the pre-independence days and tried his hands at different business ventures. He was a Gandhian who preferred wearing khadi and conducted his business with impeccable values. Most significantly, he was a philanthropist who believed that the wealth you create must be used for the betterment of the country and society. Thus, he started his contribution to education by opening a school 82 years ago. So, as you study here over the next two years, I will urge you to remember the glorious legacy of this institution, imbibe and live by the principles he embodied. As you begin, it's natural to feel both excited and a bit apprehensive about what lies ahead. I encourage you to make the most of the next two years by fully engaging with our exceptional academic programme, seizing the opportunity to interact with industry leaders in the commercial capital of India, and utilising the sports facility to build physical fitness and mental resilience. Most importantly, take this time to cultivate a vast network of fellow students, alumni and faculty mentors who will support and guide you. I hope this handbook will be a useful reference during your stint here. I wish you all the very best and look forward to growing together!

title: General Rules and Regulations
content: KJSIM upholds a strong framework of rules and regulations designed to foster academic excellence, personal integrity, and a respectful campus environment. Students are expected to adhere to high standards of honesty, maintain regular attendance, dress appropriately, and use technology responsibly. From academic conduct to campus dining and communication, these guidelines ensure a disciplined and inclusive atmosphere conducive to learning and professional growth. 1. Academic Integrity: Honesty: All students are expected to maintain the highest standards of academic honesty. Cheating, plagiarism, or any form of academic dishonesty is strictly prohibited and will result in disciplinary action. Examinations: No unauthorised materials or communication are permitted during examinations. Any attempt to cheat will lead to strict disciplinary measures. Assignments: All submitted work must be original and properly referenced. In group assignments, the contributions of each member must be clearly reflected. 2. Attendance and Participation: Class Attendance: Regular attendance is mandatory. Students are expected to attend all classes, participate actively, and be punctual. Leave of Absence: For medical absences, a valid medical certificate must be provided. Participation: Active participation in class discussions, projects, and other activities is highly encouraged, and in many cases, it contributes to the final grade. 3. Dress Code: Professional Attire: Students are expected to dress in professional attire for classes, presentations, and events unless otherwise specified. Casual Attire: Casual dress is acceptable outside of formal events but should remain appropriate at all times. 4. Campus Dining Facilities: Vegetarian Menu: All meals served within campus dining facilities, including the cafeteria and canteen, are strictly vegetarian. Prohibited Items: Non-vegetarian food items, including eggs, meat, fish, and poultry, are prohibited within campus dining premises. 5. Use of Technology: Laptops and Devices: Laptops and other electronic devices may be in class solely for academic purposes. The use of social media or other non- academic activities during class is not allowed. Social Media: Students are expected to exercise responsibility when using social media, as their behaviour reflects on themselves and the Institute. Email Communication: Students are required to regularly check their university email for official communications and respond promptly. Prohibited and Inappropriate Websites: Accessing inappropriate or blocked websites related to gambling, hacking, hate speech, pornography, illegal activities, or any content that violates ethical, legal, or institutional guidelines is strictly prohibited. 7. Placement Assistance: Utilisation: Students are encouraged to make full use of placement assistance services for internships, job placements, and career guidance. Professionalism: When representing the Institute in any professional setting, students must adhere to the highest standards of professionalism. Adherence to Placement Policy: Non-adherence to the placement policy will lead to disciplinary action, leading to debarment from internships and final placements. 8. Compliance: Handbook Adherence: Students are required to abide by all rules and regulations set forth in this handbook. Failure to comply may lead to disciplinary action. Updates: Students are responsible for staying informed about any changes or updates to these rules and regulations. Declarations: Students are required to ensure the accuracy of all information provided in any declarations made during their time at the institute. Any false or incorrect declarations may result in termination from the programme.

title: KJSIM Code Of Conduct For Students
content: KJSIM upholds the values and principles instilled by our founder. In order to preserve the integrity of the campus, all students are expected to adhere to the following guidelines: 1. Anti-Ragging Policy: KJSIM enforces a Zero Tolerance policy towards any form of ragging. Ragging is strictly prohibited under Indian law, specifically the UGC Act of 1956 and the Indian Penal Code (IPC). The aim of this policy is to eliminate, prevent, and prohibit any instances of ragging on campus or within hostel premises. Should any such incident occur, students are required to report it immediately to the concerned authorities. 2. Prevention of Sexual Harassment: KJSIM maintains a Zero Tolerance policy towards sexual harassment in any form and is committed to creating a safe and healthy environment that is free from prejudice, gender bias, and harassment. Every individual at KJSIM, including students, faculty, and staff, has the right to be treated with dignity and respect. Sexual harassment is a serious offence, punishable by law, and will be dealt with sternly by the Institute. 3. Student Conduct and Responsibility: Students residing on campus are expected to conduct themselves with maturity, responsibility, and accountability throughout their stay. It is also essential that students uphold academic integrity. 4. Identification and Security: Students must carry their identity cards at all times while on campus. Security personnel, as well as faculty and administrative staff, may request to see your ID at any time. Failure to present your ID may result in disciplinary action. 5. Vehicle Policy: Bringing vehicles into the campus without prior permission is strictly prohibited. Students are advised to seek approval from the administration before bringing vehicles to the campus. 6 Respect for Property: Students are expected to respect and maintain the property of the Institute and the campus at all times. Any damage caused to campus facilities will result in disciplinary measures. It is also essential that students uphold a high standard of personal conduct on campus. 7. Prohibition of Sales: Students are not permitted to engage in the sale of goods on campus. Any items brought with the intention to sell will be confiscated by the Institute. 8.Substance Abuse: The possession or use of alcohol, drugs, tobacco, or any illegal substances is strictly prohibited on campus. 9. Commitment to Ethical Standards: Every member of the KJSIM community is expected to uphold the ethical standards of the Institute, ensuring that our reputation and values are maintained. As part of a larger academic and social community, each individual bears the responsibility to act with integrity and respect.

title: Parivartan - Enabling Transformation Journeys
content: KJSIM has put in place an integrated dynamic framework, 'Parivartan', to enable students' transformation during their 2-year MBA journey. It has two key pillars: Nurturing Potential (NP) and the Student Progression Index (SPI). 1. Nurturing Potential: Nurturing Potential helps students recognise their innate capabilities or competencies and take responsibility for their development. Based on research, KJSIM has identified seven competencies (listed below) that are critical to succeed in any function, along with differentiating competencies required to succeed in specific roles in the professional world of work: 1. Working with People (Collaboration) 2. Adhering to Principles and Values 3. Presenting and Communicating Information 4. Analysing 5. Learning and Researching 6. Delivering Results and Meeting Customer Expectations 7. Adapting and Responding to Change The students are assessed on these competencies using a psychometric test in association with a consulting firm. The students receive a self-evaluation report which highlights their relative strengths and development areas. The designated mentors for the students will receive the SHL report of their mentees and facilitate the students' development over the two years of their MBA journey through classroom sessions and various other initiatives. 2. Student Progression Index (SPI): KJSIM believes that true transformation happens through academic efforts within classrooms as well as experiences beyond the classroom. Student Progression Index is designed to encourage student participation in areas which enable their holistic development. The parameters for measuring student progression index are: 1. Academics (CGPA) – Students' academic performance (CGPA) constitutes 50% weightage. The CGPA gets averaged at every progressive semester. 2. Deep Reflective Learning through Ken & Economist (DRLK&E) – To inculcate the habit of reading and reflection, selected articles from The Ken and The Economist are shared with students on a weekly basis, along with possible reflective questions. Students are evaluated by their designated mentors, and it constitutes 15% weightage of which 7.5% for attending and 7.5% for worthy contributions in the broad discussion. Students are expected to participate in 5 discussions in each of the semesters in the first year. 3. Careers & Conversations (C&C) - Eminent speakers from different walks of life share their personal and professional voyages. This platform aims to provide ample learning opportunities through real-life stories. C&C constitutes 5% weightage for attending 3 sessions in 2 years. 4. Certifications – There is considerable evidence that industry-led certifications increase the probability of being hired with a higher salary. KJSIM encourages students to discuss with their mentors and enrol for industry-led certifications or pursue relevant certification to bridge the gap between academic theory and practical industry skills. one industry-led certification or three skill-based courses from Coursera, Udemy, edX or a similar learning platform over a period of 2 years (1 per semester), will constitute 10% weightage. 5. Competitions - KJSIM drives students to participate in case studies, business plans and sports competitions to complement academic learning and enhance their holistic development. Participation in two such competitions or winning a competition will constitute 10% weightage. 6. Health & Fitness - Maintaining good health and fitness levels can significantly enhance academic performance, stress management, and overall quality of life. Students enrol on fitness and sports sessions to keep themselves healthy and fit. Health & Fitness constitutes 10% weightage, of which 7% is for participation in any sport and 3% for performance evaluation. This will be calculated every semester. This framework underscores the following objectives for students: • Helps students gain self-awareness about their strengths and areas of development • Helps students to get an additional lens to make judicious choices relating to specialisation and career. • Instil habits and behaviours that will help to continuously adapt, evolve and thrive in the dynamic workplace. Parivartan Portal will be accessible to students through their Somaiya mail ID. This portal will enable students to access their fitment scores, set development goals through Parivartan action planner, choose components of SPI that will help them to fulfil those goals, interact with their mentors and be in the know of their progression score. At the end of every semester (for 3 semesters), the top 25 SPI scorers are duly rewarded by the institute.

title: Programmes Offered and Course Structure
content: KJSIM offers the following programmes: • Doctoral Programme (Ph.D) in Management • MBA • MBA Healthcare Management • MBA Sports Management • MBA Executive • Masters in Computer Applications. Masters of Business Administration: Semester I includes courses like Business Statistics, Cost Management and Control, Design Thinking and Entrepreneurship, Financial Reporting and Analysis, Marketing Management I, Microeconomics, Operations Management, Organizational Behaviour, Spreadsheet Modelling, Decision Science. Mandatory Audit Courses include Business Communication, Business Sustainability and Business Ethics, Health and Fitness. Semester II includes courses like Business Research Methods, Data Science Using Python, Financial Management, Human Resource Management, Logistics and Supply Chain Management, Macroeconomics, Marketing Management II, Project Management, Strategic Management, and one Elective. Mandatory Audit Courses include Business Communication, Business Sustainability and Business Ethics, Health and Fitness, Leadership Development. Master of Business Administration-Healthcare Management: Semester I includes courses like Business Communication & Report Writing, Epidemiology and Patient Care, Fundamentals of Finance, Fundamentals of Health Economics, Fundamentals of Marketing, Healthcare Marketing, Hospital Administration, Organizational Behaviour, Spreadsheet for Data Analytics, Statistical Analysis. Semester II includes courses like Cost & Managerial Accounting, Design Thinking and Entrepreneurship Management, Digital Marketing in Healthcare, Healthcare Services Management, Human Resource Management, Macroeconomic Aspects of Health Sector, Pharma and Biotech Management, Research Methodology, Operations and Supply Chain Management, and one Elective. Semester III includes courses like Advanced Analytics in Healthcare Using R & Python, Financial Management, International Business and Medical Tourism, Medico-Legal Environment and Ethics in Healthcare, Quality Management in Healthcare and Six Sigma, Retail Management in Healthcare. Summer Internship Project is also included. Semester IV includes courses like Customer Relationship Management and Integrated Marketing Communication, Emerging Technologies and New Product Development in Healthcare, Health Insurance & Financing, Healthcare in Rural Markets and NGO Management, Preventive Healthcare and Demographic Science, Strategic Brand Management. Capstone Project is also included. MBA-Sports Management (MBA-SM): Semester I includes courses like Business Communication, Human Behaviour and HR Practices in Sports Management, Introduction to Sports and Sports Industry, Management Accounting in Sports, Statistics in Sports Management, Advanced Excel, Sports Economics, Sports Marketing and Sponsorship Management, Sports Structure, Development and Governance, Sports Ventures, Innovation and CSR. Semester II includes courses like Applied Sports Marketing Research, Financial Management in Sports, Sports Analytics Management, Sports Events and Facilities Management, Sports Project Management, Technology in Sports, Legal and Ethics in Sports Management, Sports Operations & Supply Chain Management, Strategic Management. Winter Internship I is also included.

title: Major - Minor Policy
content: KJSIM-SVU/ Selection of Major-Minor – Batch 2025-27/Version 1.0/2025-26. Year 1: Foundational Learning and Industry Exposure. In the first year, students are introduced to all core functional areas of management through a comprehensive set of foundational courses. By the middle of the year, students will have developed an understanding of various disciplines within management and gained an overview of the industry. This is achieved through classroom learning as well as co-curricular engagements such as panel discussions, guest lectures, competitions, and capstone projects. Year 2: Specialisation through Majors and Minors. Major Specialisation: In the second year, students have the opportunity to specialise by selecting a major in specific management areas such as Business Analytics & Data Science, Finance, Human Resources (People Development), Marketing, and Operations. The major builds on the foundational knowledge acquired in the first year, offering ten specialised courses spread across the third and fourth semesters. Semester 3 includes six courses, while Semester 4 comprises four courses, collectively totalling to 30 credits (1,000 marks). A minimum of 20 students is required for a major to be offered. Objective of Major: The major enables students to develop in-depth expertise in their chosen area of specialisation. Through focused study, students build the competence necessary to excel in their field and prepare for industry-specific roles. Outcomes of Choosing a Major: Selecting a major equips students with specialised knowledge and skills, enhancing their readiness for the corporate world. It allows for a deeper understanding of the functional area, ultimately influencing students' short-term career decisions and improving their employability. The following five majors are offered to the students: 1. Business Analytics & Data Science 2. Finance 3. Human Resources (People Development) 4. Marketing 5. Operations. Minor Specialisation: Alongside the major, students also select a minor that complements their primary area of study. Objective of Choosing a Minor: The objective of the minor is to complement the student's selected major by offering additional knowledge in a related or entirely new area of management. It aims to provide a broader understanding of management disciplines beyond the student's primary specialisation. In doing so, the minor enables students to gain both practical and conceptual insights into an additional domain, thereby enhancing their interdisciplinary competence. Outcomes of Choosing a Minor: The minor enhances a student's overall academic and professional profile by equipping them with expertise in a secondary area of management. This additional specialisation broadens their perspective, fosters versatility, and prepares them for roles that demand cross-functional knowledge. By engaging with minor courses, students become more adaptable in dynamic work environments and are better positioned to pursue a wider range of career opportunities, both within and beyond their primary area of focus. The minor typically consists of four full-credit courses, evenly distributed across Semesters 3 and 4. However, in the case of Human Resources (People Development), the minor includes seven half-credit courses. Each minor course carries 100 marks, totalling 400 marks (12 credits) over the two semesters. A minimum of 20 students is required to offer any minor. Minors are offered in the following domains: 1. Business Analytics & Data Science 2. Business Economics 3. Finance 4. Human Resources (People Development) 5. Marketing 6. Operations 7. Strategy and Consulting. Students Can Choose the Major and Minor Based on: 1. Work experience 2. Competency 3. Interest, Inclination & Passion 4. External environment. Note: • Students are not permitted to select the same area of specialisation for both their Major and Minor. • Once selected, requests for changes to the Major or Minor will not be entertained. • Students will be assigned to divisions through a randomised process. As a result, they may not remain in the same division as in the first year, and division groupings may differ in the second year. Requests for changes in division assignments will not be entertained. • The Institute will allocate the Major and Minor specialisations to students who fail to submit their preferences within the stipulated timeline.

title: Attendance Policy
content: KJSIM-SVU/ Attendance Policy/Version 1.0/2025-2026. Revised on 31st March, 2025; valid for all programs started in academic year 2024-25 & 2025-26). At K J Somaiya Institute of Management (KJSIM), full attendance to the classes and coming on time is of utmost importance. This policy governs how attendance defaulters and late-comers are dealt with. Attendance carries 10 marks per course and will be a part of the Internal Assessment of the course. Internal assessment marks are 50 in case of full credit courses- bifurcated as follows: 40 marks for all other components and 10 marks for attendance. Marks will be deducted as below - per course attendance: • If the percentage of sessions attended is in decimal form, then it will be rounded off. • If the number of sessions in a course is only 15, then the minimum number of sessions to be attended is 11. • Exceptions: Medical reasons requiring hospitalisation or confinement, representing the institution in academic and sports competitions requiring students to be absent from the campus, or any other impactful activity for the institute, or exceptional situations where students had to attend placement-related activities. Absence due to placement-related activity should be approved by the Placement Department. • For medical reasons, a valid doctor's certificate and hospital discharge summary are required. For all others, a pre-approval by the process owner, who shall be informed immediately via email by the student or family member. These documents must be submitted to the programme office within three working days after joining. Late submissions shall not be entertained. Please note: Submission of fake medical certificates will result in disciplinary action. If a student fails to meet the attendance criteria repeatedly (attendance defaulter in multiple courses or attendance defaulter across semesters), the institute reserves the right to take punitive action, which may include not being allowed to sit for the exams. Attendance Marks allotted: 90-100%: 10, 80-89%: 8, 70%-79%: 6, <70%: 0. • Students' attendance records will be available on the MyAccount portal. In addition, on a rolling basis, defaulters will be informed that they do not meet the minimum 70% attendance and are likely to lose 10 marks in the internal assessment. A copy of such email may be sent to the student's parents/guardians. Students are expected to be in the classroom before a lecture is scheduled to start. Coming late to a class disrupts the flow of the class, both from the faculty's and fellow students' perspective. Students who are late by more than 15 minutes shall not be allowed to attend the class and shall be marked absent. For other latecomers (students late for a class less than 15 minutes), faculty may choose to: a) Grant attendance at their discretion b) Not allow the student into the class c) Mark the student absent, but allow the student to sit in the class d) Allow students who procure the 'Attendance Exemption Note' as mentioned below. A student is to come back with 'Attendance Exemption Note' duly signed and approved by the Director's Nominee. The forms will be available at the Admin office. (This can be done a maximum of two times in a particular course). Only the Programme Officers are authorised to make changes, if any, to student attendance records based on authorised exemptions (Placement, Admissions, Sports, and other teams shall provide exemption data to the PO team). The faculty is responsible for marking the attendance during the session only. The Programme Office will send the final attendance data sheet (after making changes as per the attendance policy) to the faculty concerned, who will include the marks in their internal mark sheet and send it to the exam department.

title: Examination and Assessment Policy
content: Examination Rules for Eligibility, Passing Criteria, Grace Marks, and Grievance Redressal. PG Programmes – Effective AY 2024-25. (Refer to Examination Manual – Document No. 255 – for comprehensive details). Name of the College/Department/Institute: K J Somaiya Institute of Management. Section of Exam Manual Description of Policy Rule/Policy: Eligibility for Admission to Subsequent Years: 38 Admission to SY • For two years PG programmes – no such eligibility criteria. 38 Admission to TY • For three years PG programmes – no such eligibility criteria. 38 Admission to LY • Not Applicable. CA (Test + IA) and ESE Distribution and Passing Criteria: 34, 35/Annexure II Percentage CA (Test + IA) for Theory Course • Separate Head of Passing – 50% Passing • Combined/Overall Passing Percentage (CA+ESE) – 50% • In case of some courses, with approval of the Academic Excellence Committee there might be no ETE conducted, and the course may be assessed fully on a CA basis with the same passing percentage. • Faculty shall submit marks in two columns CA and CA in lieu of ETE. 34, 35/Annexure II Percentage ESE for Theory Course • Separate Head of Passing – 50% Passing • Combined/Overall Passing Percent age (CA+ESE) – 50% • In case of some courses, withapproval of the Academic Excellence Committee there might be no ETE conducted, and the course may be assessed fully on a CA basis with the same passing percentage. Faculty shall submit marks in two columns CA and CA in lieu of ETE. 34, 35/Annexure II P/O/TUT/P and O/S/Project – any evaluation other than CA and ESE of Theory Course • 50% passing • Separate head of passing. Grace Marks – Internal: 37 Internal Grace Marks CA of Theory Course Not applicable for all examinations – R/KT/SUP/re-examination of R/KT/SUP. Except in case of courses where CA is in lieu of ETE. 37 Additional Internal Grace Marks for CA for PwD/Sports/NCC/NSS/Home Guard Not applicable for all examinations - R/KT/SUP/re-examination of R/KT/SUP. 37 Internal Grace Marks for P/O/TUT/P and O/S/Project – any evaluation other than CA and ESE of Theory Course Not applicable for all examinations - R/KT/SUP/re-examination of R/KT/SUP. Except in case of courses where CA is in lieu of ETE. 37 Additional Internal Grace Marks for PwD/sports/NCC/NSS/home guard for P/O/TUT/P and O/S/Project - any evaluation other than CA and ESE of Theory Course Not applicable for all examinations – R/KT/SUP/re-examination of R/KT/SUP. 37 Internal Grace Marks for ESE theory R/KT/SUP/Re-Examination of R/KT/SUP Examination Not applicable for all examinations – R/KT/SUP/Re-Examination of R/KT/SUP. 37 Additional Internal Grace Marks PwD/Sports/NCC/NSS/Home Guard for ESE theory R/KT/SUP/Re-Examination of R/KT/SUP Examination Not applicable for all examinations – R/KT/SUP/Re-Examination of R/KT/SUP. 37 Both Internal & External Grace Marks are given to passing head No external grace marks are given wherever applicable. Section of Exam Manual Description of Policy Rule/Policy: 37 Re-verification of ESE theory R/KT/SUP/Re-Examination of R/KT/SUP Examination Any no. of courses the student can apply in prescribed format. 37 Re-assessment of ESE theory R/KT/SUP/Re-Examination of R/KT/SUP Examination • Any no. of courses the student can apply in prescribed format. 24, 37 Re-assessment of CA/P/O/TUT/P and O/S/Project – any evaluation of R/KT/SUP/Re-Examination of R/KT/SUP Examination other than CA and ESE Theory Course • Re-assessment is applicable to the theory, lab-based examination against application in prescribed format. Practical exams are not eligible for re-assessment. • Internal grace marks are not applicable. Grace Marks – External: 37 External grace marks for theory/CA/ESE/P/O/TUT/P and O/S/Project – any evaluation for R/KT/SUP/Re-Examination of R/KT/SUP Examination - any head passing Grace marks may be awarded in the following situations: • End Semester Examination (ESE) • End Term Examination (ETE) • Courses where Continuous Assessment (CA) replaces ESE/ETE • Reassessment cases However, grace marks are not applicable to students who have failed in more than two subjects. The maximum grace marks granted will be 8, which can be applied to a single course or distributed between two courses. Grace marks are only awarded if the student passes the exam after the application of these marks. Please note that grace marks do not apply to Summer Internships or Master Theses. 37 Grace Marks for PwD Student • 3% of maximum marks to pass the semester examination. Additional Grace Marks: 37 Yes, benefits will be granted to students who meet the following criteria: 36 Section of Exam Manual Description of Policy Rule/Policy: Additional Grace Marks for Sports for R/Re-Examination of R • SVU Team Members: Students who are members of teams that reach the quarter-finals in All India University-level intercollegiate competitions. OR • Top Eight in Individual Sports: Students who secure one of the top eight positions in individual sports events conducted by the university. OR • Representation in Sports: Students who represent the university at the interstate, zonal, national, or international level. • Students must submit the required merit certificate issued by the organising secretary, along with an application endorsed by the relevant authorities (Principal, Director, Head of Institution, or College Head), two weeks prior to the End Semester Examination. This application must then be forwarded by the head of the concerned section to the Examination Department before the examination. • The benefit applies to one semester examination per academic year, typically for the even semester examination. 37 Section of Exam Manual Description of Policy Rule/Policy: Additional Grace Marks for Sports for R/Re-Examination of R • Grace marks are for passing the subject/course/passing head. • Total grace marks given for passing /exemption for subject/course/passing head shall not be more than 10. • In case of failure in one or more heads of passing an examination, the benefit of grace marks shall be granted to an extent of 5% of maximum marks allotted to a subject/course/passing head. • The grace marks will be granted for the purpose of claiming exemptions in the subject/course/passing head; it shall not exceed 5% of the maximum marks allotted to subject/course/passing head. • These grace marks will be additional. • Grace marks for overall grade improvement • The maximum of 10 grace marks shall be added to the grand total. • In the case of CBGS system 0.10 will be added to final grade to GPA even if the benefit is given to the student for passing the subject/course/passing head of the said semester R/Re-Examination of R examination. The benefit shall be granted for one semester per academic year, provided the student submits an application in the prescribed format, accompanied by certification from the competent authority. 38 Section of Exam Manual Description of Policy Rule/Policy: 37 Additional Grace Marks for NSS/NCC/Home Guards for R/Re-Examination of R • Yes, against applications received from the concerned student/s. • Students who have enrolled as member of NCC and have satisfactorily completed NCC training programme during the academic year as certified by the competent authorities/Principal/Director/HOI/Head OR • The students who have enrolled for the Home Guard and have satisfactorily completed
